trivia_bullets.text: Khans of Tarkir (released 2014-09-26) was the first set of the Tarkir block and the last three-set block in Magic history. Feed the Clan belongs to the Temur Frontier (GUR), the isolationist mountain clan that values the strength of living things. [Scryfall], [Wizards: Mechanics of Khans of Tarkir]

Feed the Clan is a {1}{G} instant that gains 5 life baseline, or 10 life with Ferocious (controlling a creature with power 4 or greater). It is a consistent Pauper Mono-Green Stompy sideboard staple for shutting down Burn matchups. [Scryfall], [Pauper Stompy guide via Team Hold Priority]

"The Temur believe three things only are needed in life: a hot fire, a full belly, and a strong companion." [Scryfall] — Speaker unnamed; encapsulates Temur anti-civilization ethos (survival, community, raw strength without hierarchy) and mirrors the card's mechanical identity.

Illustrated by Winona Nelson (140+ MTG cards). Nelson was profiled in Hipsters of the Coast's Arting Around column in July 2015; she describes connecting emotionally to each assignment rather than approaching illustration purely technically. [Hipsters of the Coast: Arting Around — Winona Nelson Interview, 2015]

Legal in Pauper, Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, Vintage, Commander, and Historic. EDHREC reports 5,722 Commander decks include the card, paired most often with Heroes' Reunion and Rest for the Weary. [EDHREC], [Scryfall]
Feed the Clan received one reprint: The List (PLST) on 2019-11-07 as collector number KTK-132. No draftable reprint set. [Scryfall prints search]
